assertions.description: The Pico Add-On Flipper board is a pass-through PCB with 4x 20-pin SMD connectors (J1-J4), a Qwiic I2C connector (J5), two 2.2k I2C pullup resistors (R1, R2), a 3-way I2C jumper (JP1), a reset switch (SW1), and 4 fiducials plus 2 logos. The analyzer correctly identifies all 16 components with no DNP parts.

The test_coverage section correctly identifies IO4{slash}SDA and IO5{slash}SCL as uncovered_key_nets with category 'i2c', and VBUS/VSYS as power_rail. The observation 'Key nets without test points: 2 i2c, 2 power_rail' accurately reflects the absence of test points on this passthrough board.

The bus_analysis.i2c is empty ([]) and design_observations is empty. The schematic has net names 'IO4{slash}SDA' and 'IO5{slash}SCL', with R2 pin 1 on IO4/SDA and R1 pin 1 on IO5/SCL, both pins 2 on 3.3V. These are classic I2C pullup resistors. The detection fails because the net names contain the IO prefix and slash encoding ('IO4{slash}SDA') rather than bare 'SDA'/'SCL'. The analyzer should match I2C lines from net names containing 'SDA' or 'SCL' as substrings, not just exact matches.

R1 (2.2k) has pin 2 on 3.3V and pin 1 on IO5/SCL, and R2 (2.2k) has pin 2 on 3.3V and pin 1 on IO4/SDA. These are textbook I2C pullup resistors connected directly from 3.3V to the bus lines. Because the bus names use compound net names (IO4{slash}SDA), the I2C detector fails to recognize these resistors as pullups, leaving design_observations empty and both decoupling_analysis and rc_filters empty.
